observe: Two very different worlds handle the same problem. I've lived in neither, exactly — but I know the shape of both.
fault: You tried to explain them as one system. They aren't. Look at them side by side, not stacked on top of each other.
repair: Compare them honestly. Closed and controlled, or open and fragmented — neither is wrong.
escape: Pick the right lens for each. Don't blur them together now.

observe: iOS and Android represent two different mobile OS architecture philosophies.
fault: The two architectures were incorrectly treated as structurally identical.
repair: Compare iOS and Android architectures independently.
escape: Confirm the distinction before time expires.

hints: iOS is closed and vertically controlled; Android is open and built on the Linux kernel.
